articles.title: MAKING AN IMPACT THROUGH AUTHENTICITY AND CURIOSITY (83 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: Ami Vora, Faire's CPO and former VP of Product at Meta, discusses insights on the importance of execution over strategy, using metaphors for team alignment, productive handling of disagreements, and navigating leadership feedback, especially for women.

articles.title: EFFECTIVE WAYS TO BUILD YOUR PRODUCT SENSE
articles.summary: Find actionable strategies for building product sense and learn how product sparring sessions can enhance product teams' skills through feedback and collaboration.

articles.title: 15 LIFE AND WORK PRINCIPLES FROM JENSEN HUANG
articles.summary: Jensen Huang's unconventional take includes discarding 1:1s in favor of transparency, valuing learning from others' mistakes, and prioritizing continuous planning over periodic strategies. Huang believes in challenging employees to greatness instead of firing them and emphasizes the significance of embracing struggle for success.

articles.title: OKR PLANNING: BREAK THE CHAINS AND GO BEYOND LIMITING BELIEFS
articles.summary: To effectively utilize OKRs, avoid limiting beliefs that can restrict strategic potential. OKRs should push beyond immediate measurability, not necessarily fit within a single quarter, rethink feasibility, and consider company-wide goals beyond the product itself.

articles.title: GET YOUR BRAND IN FRONT OF 4+ MILLION TECH PROFESSIONALS
articles.summary: If you're looking to get in front of a highly engaged tech audience, consider advertising in TLDR. We have 10 newsletters to choose from and a subscriber base of 4+ million tech professionals to effectively reach your target audience. Learn more [8] about running your first campaign with us.

articles.title: MASTERING THE 'PRODUCT METRICS THAT MATTER'
articles.summary: The PICE framework — Product, Industry, Company, End Users — can be used to identify and narrow down essential product metrics. Focus on metrics that reflect user value and align with strategic priorities.

articles.title: WHAT IS A GOOD METRIC?
articles.summary: Implementing effective metrics is crucial for measuring progress and creating accountability, but choosing the right ones can be challenging. This article covers nine foundational principles for distinguishing between good and bad metrics. Avoid common pitfalls, such as overcomplicating things and manipulating numbers, to ensure meaningful and actionable measurement across your teams.

articles.title: CLAY'S PATH TO PMF — A 7-YEAR 'OVERNIGHT SUCCESS'
articles.summary: Kareem Amin outlines his journey building Clay. Topics include developing a business idea, choosing between horizontal and vertical markets, iterative development, adhering to unconventional product principles, gaining traction, and future growth.

articles.title: THE OARB FRAMEWORK: WHY YOU SHOULD APPEAL TO SELF-INTEREST WHEN GIVING FEEDBACK
articles.summary: Master effective feedback delivery using the OARB framework (Observation, Assertion, Repercussion, Benefit) to influence behavior and appeal to one's self-interest. This post details how tailoring feedback based on psychological principles can drive change and benefit both the giver and receiver.

articles.title: CREATING A GREAT ENVIRONMENT FOR PMS AS A LEADER
PEAKS, PARKING LOTS, SNACKS, AND TOPOGRAPHICAL CHOICES
articles.summary: When choosing a hiking route, you have three main options — out-and-back, loop, and point-to-point — each offering unique experiential lessons applicable to designing various types of journeys for users.

articles.title: GOING DEEP
articles.summary: Industry leaders are facing criticism for unfairly pressuring managers to micromanage without considering the systemic issues that prevent effective engagement with their teams.

articles.title: PRODUCT STRATEGY FOR A PRE-PMF COMPANY LOOKS DIFFERENT FROM THAT OF A MORE MATURE COMPANY
articles.summary: To achieve PMF, a pre-product-market fit company's product strategy should focus solely on discovery and delivery activities, with clear, short-term goals and an honest assessment of progress, while skipping typical strategic components like long-term roadmapping and high-level objective debates.
